name: West Sumatra (Indonesia)

colors.symbolism: the niniak mamak (clan elders), representing wisdom and the system of customary law (adat)
the cadiak pandai (intellectuals), representing courage and bravery
the alim ulama (religious scholars), representing the dignity and the Islamic faith of the Minangkabau

description: Three equal vertical stripes of black (hoist), red (centre), and yellow (fly). The flag is also known as the Marawa Tigo Warna ('three-coloured banner').
history: The black-red-yellow tricolour (Marawa Tigo) is a centuries-old emblem of the Minangkabau people of the Pagaruyung sultanate in West Sumatra. The colours stand for the three pillars of traditional Minangkabau society - clan elders, intellectuals, and religious scholars (the 'tungku tigo sajarangan'). The provincial flag of West Sumatra (Sumatera Barat) was adopted by the Provincial Regulation No. 1/1969 and confirms this design.

features.symbolism: The Marawa Tigo: a banner of three vertical stripes - black, red, and yellow - that is the historic flag of the Minangkabau people of West Sumatra, said to embody the threefold authority of the adat (tradition), the religion, and the ulama (clerics).
